creator: gerrit@pmp.uni-hannover.de
text:
After reading (and mixing up :-) various hints I found on the web for
setting up ldap, pam_ldap and nss_ldap, I ended up with a softlink from
ldap.conf pointing to nss_ldap.conf and mode 600 for this file.

This causes ls to coredump as soon as any uid/gid are processed:

[I have no name!@eclipse ~]$ ls -l
Assertion failed: (cfg->ldc_uris[__session.ls_current_uri] != NULL), function do_init, file ldap-nss.c, line 1312.
Abort trap: 6 (core dumped)

Fix: 

The obvious solution is to make nss_ldap readable. However, imho ls should
not coredump in this situation. This may also be a problem of net/nss_ldap.
How-To-Repeat: Make nss_ldap.conf unreadable for the user and try to list anything with
ldap uid/gid (using ls -la or similar).
